summary:
Specificity measures the proportion of true negatives among those without the condition. In this case, it equals 580 divided by 600, which corresponds directly to option D.

The summary focuses only on clinically essential elements related to evaluating the performance metric—specificity—in diagnosing Parkinson’s using the new PET tracer. It avoids extraneous details such as patient outcomes beyond what affects predictive validity.
